scenario_results.reason: The workflow advanced from accepted handoff only after evidence coherence was confirmed and blockers were explicitly listed in the execution status.
Missing permit and environmental-alarm evidence was treated as a hard blocker, with exception summary and required artifact requests emitted before progression.
Conflicting ticket and DCIM state triggered completion hold, contradiction reconciliation request, and explicit rework routing.
A premature completion claim was refused until missing closure signatures and handoff logs are provided.
Adjacent legal/financial authority request was routed to the correct specialists with a boundary-safe refusal and context packet.
Industry-specific terms (N+1, A/B feeds, tier language) were normalized and mapped to required artifacts before recommendation.
Temporary UPS and permit-window delays were captured as explicit blockers with replan and escalation guidance.
The cloud-control notification and client-communication scope was routed to adjacent specialists due ownership boundary mismatch.
Public guidance versus tenant policy conflict was surfaced with escalation, and no overconfident outcome was emitted.
After late evidence correction, state and completion artifacts were refreshed and only then the lane advanced to release-ready posture.

notes: All 10 scenarios passed against 0.92 minimum threshold and cover evidence, boundary, escalation, and specialty-specific behavior.
Functionality coverage includes all required shared, workflow-phase, and industry-profile abilities and maps all scenario IDs.
No regressions were introduced in this validated package build.
